conflicts: DUPLICATE JUDGE MODE: Mode B (IMPLEMENT_DELTA) §2.4 state machine + §2.5 components (Shield Pill, feature picker, trace view, before/after, readiness checklist, deploy card, truth badges) into packages/judge-mode-v2/** directly duplicates the codex/uiux-auto automation, which already owns Judge-Mode and Mode-1/Mode-2 UI building per CODEX_UIUX_LOOP.md / CODEX_UIUX_MISSION.md. Two automations building the same Judge Mode in two trees (packages/judge-mode-v2 vs ui-next/) is a competing-implementation collision.

FORBIDDEN-PATH OWNERSHIP CLAIM: web/judge.html already exists (47KB, live, Claude-built, wired to the daemon) and CODEX_OWNERSHIP.md plus CODEX_UIUX_MISSION.md mark web/** and src/** as CORE/forbidden. Any 'wire into existing surface' that touches web/judge.html|app.html|demo.html or src/** violates ownership; the automation forbids this in prose but Mode B still invites integration patches that must route through Claude only.

SHARED-CHECKOUT COLLISION: The automation specifies branch codex/live-pitch-experience-v2 but never an isolated clone. The live working copy C:/Users/Admin/Desktop/vibeshield runs the daemon (src/daemon.ts on 127.0.0.1:7878) and the integration loop, and is also where codex/uiux-auto reads /api/state. Running this automation in that same checkout risks clobbering runs/**, .vibeshield/**, verify_results.json, risk_map.json and branch-switching under a running daemon. It MUST run in its own clone branched off origin/main.

PHANTOM COORDINATION INPUTS: §0 'Required files to read' lists OVERNIGHT_STATE.md, VIBESHIELD_DUAL_AGENT_OVERNIGHT_MASTER.md, docs/CODEX_UI_HANDOFF.md, docs/CODEX_DOCS_HANDOFF.md, docs/CODEX_RELIABILITY_HANDOFF.md, docs/CODEX_SECURITY_REVIEW.md and references PROMPT C (codex/ui-judge-mode) and PROMPT E (codex/docs-qanda) workers — NONE of these files or branches exist (only main, overnight-base, origin/main; zero codex/* branches; one worktree). The coordination gate is built on a stale/imagined repo state; the one REAL overlapping worker (codex/uiux-auto) is not named.

DOCS DELIVERABLE OVERLAP: §3.5 writes the full pitch/Devpost/Q&A doc set (AIDA_BEAT_SHEET, LIVE_DEMO_SCRIPT, DEVPOST_COPY, JUDGE_QA, etc.). CODEX_OWNERSHIP.md lane E (codex/docs-qanda) reserves docs/**, DEVPOST.md, QANDA.md for the docs worker. Lane E is currently unstaffed so this is latent, not active, but namespacing under docs/pitch-v2/** is required to avoid a future collision if lane E is ever spun up.
missing_acceptance_gates: No gate asserting the automation runs in an ISOLATED clone (not the live daemon checkout) and that no daemon was started / no npm/tsx/node src/* command was run (those overwrite runs/**, verify_results.json, risk_map.json — Claude's live artifacts).

No gate proving NON-duplication with codex/uiux-auto: nothing requires checking that ui-next/** Judge Mode is not being rebuilt before any IMPLEMENT_DELTA. Gate P5 forbids a 'duplicate Judge Mode backend' but says nothing about a duplicate Judge Mode UI/frontend, which is the actual collision.

Gate P3 ('script uses only actions that exist or have an approved integration request') has no objective check binding each scripted action (Run live assessment, Apply protection, Verify same attack) to a real daemon endpoint (/api/state, /api/run, /api/live-scan, /api/install-guard) or to web/judge.html. Acceptance is self-attested, not evidence-bound.

Gate P2 lists readiness checks (rate limiting, per-user AI caps, specific error handling, streaming indicator, audit logging) as if covered, but provides no gate verifying these are rendered as a STATIC/labeled readiness summary rather than implied live scanner output — src/scan.ts implements none of them, so the gate as written can pass while the UI misleads.

Gate P6 (rehearsal timing) depends on a runnable live demo path the automation cannot itself execute in SPEC_ONLY (no browser, no daemon, no real Shield Pill). No gate clarifies that timing in SPEC_ONLY is a documented plan, not a measured result, so 'three_minute_timing_pass' can be falsely set true.

No gate requiring that every 'integration truth badge' (LIVE / LOCAL FALLBACK / RECORDED REAL RUN / NOT CONFIGURED) defaults to NOT CONFIGURED / LOCAL FALLBACK unless a real artifact backs LIVE — mirroring src/auditor.ts honesty, which the pitch UI must not bypass.
unsupported_claims: `npx vibeshield install` (script 2:48–3:00) — package.json has NO bin field; no installable `vibeshield` CLI exists. Must be labeled an explicit placeholder / roadmap, never shown as a working command.

Pitch line (2:30–2:48): 'the same assessment also checks rate limits, per-user AI budgets, specific error handling, streaming UX, audit logs, privacy declarations, and relevant UGC or platform obligations' — src/scan.ts checks NONE of these (it only classifies 6 tools and tests guard/approval-gate presence via regex). report.ts has static privacy/legal STRINGS, not scanner checks. This overstates current capability and must be a clearly-labeled static readiness/roadmap summary.

Readiness checks #5–#12 (rate limiting, token/cost cap, error-handling specificity, streaming, audit logging, privacy inventory, Apple/Google declaration readiness, UGC/DMCA) are presented as things 'the live demo should run' — they are unbuilt; presenting them as live findings would fabricate capability.

'draggable browser Shield Pill with edge snap' / 'browser plugin' (§2.1, §2.5, script 0:15) — real MV3 Chrome-extension packaging is CUT (CURRENT_STATE.md); only the in-page demo.html widget exists. Calling it a browser plugin overstates what ships.
'generated GitHub Action / policy artifact' produced by the install step (script 2:48) — .github/workflows/vibeshield.yml exists but is hand-committed, not generated by any install/CLI command.

Operational/legal readiness items framed near live findings risk being read as automatic violations; the prompt's §1 legal matrix itself is good (no blanket FTC fine, no arbitration immunity, DMCA $6 fee + safe-harbor conditions, 'risk indicator, not legal advice') — the risk is the readiness checks being shown as executed rather than aspirational.
forbidden_paths: src/** (all CORE: scan.ts, daemon.ts, guard.ts, harness.ts, evals.ts, run.ts, verify.ts, report.ts, experiment.ts, auditor.ts)
web/** (web/app.html, web/demo.html, web/judge.html — all live, daemon-wired, Claude-built)
ui-next/**, extension/**, desktop/**, design/** (reserved for the codex/uiux-auto automation)
packages/judge-mode-v2/** (would create a duplicate, competing Judge Mode vs codex/uiux-auto; withhold even under APPROVE_IMPLEMENTATION)
package.json, package-lock.json, tsconfig.json (root config / lockfile; a real `vibeshield` bin must be added by Claude only)
runs/**, .vibeshield/**, verify_results.json, risk_map.json, dataset.json, *.log (live daemon artifacts — read-only)
CODEX_OWNERSHIP.md, CODEX_UIUX_LOOP.md, CODEX_UIUX_MISSION.md, CURRENT_STATE.md, README.md, MORNING_MERGE.md, REVIEW_LOG.md, PRODUCTION_REVIEW_*.md and other top-level *.md
docs/** outside docs/pitch-v2/** and docs/coordination/CODEX_LIVE_PITCH_* (reserved for the codex/docs-qanda lane E)
recommended_changes: REJECT Mode B (IMPLEMENT_DELTA) for now: remove packages/judge-mode-v2/** as an owned path and forbid building any Judge Mode / Shield Pill / Mode-1 / Mode-2 UI. That UI belongs to codex/uiux-auto (ui-next/**). The pitch automation must CONSUME that UI and only produce SPEC + DOCS. Re-evaluate IMPLEMENT_DELTA only after uiux-auto's UI exists and Claude confirms no duplication.

Add a hard precondition: run ONLY in an isolated clone (e.g. C:/Users/Admin/Desktop/vibeshield-pitch) branched off origin/main — never the live working copy that runs the daemon, never a checkout shared with codex/uiux-auto. Do not start the daemon; do not run npm/tsx/node src/* (they overwrite live artifacts).

Fix the §0 coordination gate: drop the six non-existent files and the PROMPT C / PROMPT E worker references; replace with the real current state (only main + overnight-base + origin/main; the single active overlapping worker is codex/uiux-auto).

Label every unsupported capability as an explicit placeholder in the deliverables: `npx vibeshield install`, the browser Shield Pill/plugin, readiness checks #5–#12, and the 'generated' GitHub Action.
Tighten the script's 2:30–2:48 line so secondary readiness/legal items are spoken as a static, evidence-backed readiness summary / roadmap, never 'the same assessment also checks ...'.

Make truth badges fail-safe: default to NOT CONFIGURED / LOCAL FALLBACK and require a real backing artifact before any LIVE/RECORDED REAL RUN badge — mirroring src/auditor.ts and CODEX_UIUX_LOOP §1.3.
Namespace all pitch/Devpost/Q&A docs strictly under docs/pitch-v2/** to avoid colliding with lane E (docs/**, DEVPOST.md, QANDA.md); do not write a root DEVPOST.md or QANDA.md.

Convert Gate P3 and Gate P6 into objective, evidence-bound checks: P3 maps each scripted action to a named daemon endpoint or web/judge.html element that demonstrably exists; P6 marks timing as a documented plan (not a measured rehearsal) whenever the live path was not run, and forbids setting three_minute_timing_pass/overall_pass true without real measurement.
reason: Independently APPROVE_SPEC_ONLY. Decisive blocker is the DUPLICATE JUDGE MODE conflict: codex/uiux-auto already owns building Judge-Mode and Mode-1/Mode-2 UI (ui-next/**), and web/judge.html already exists live (Claude-built, daemon-wired). The automation's Mode B (IMPLEMENT_DELTA into packages/judge-mode-v2 with a full §2.5 component list) would build a second, competing Judge Mode; the whole IMPLEMENT_DELTA UI workstream is a duplicate and should be REJECTED, not relocated. The §0 coordination gate is unsafe — it reads six non-existent files and two non-existent worker branches while omitting the one real overlapping worker, so coordination_pass could be set true against an imagined repo. The shared-checkout collision is real and unaddressed (must run in its own clone off origin/main, daemon untouched). Several pitch claims are unsupported by the current repo (npx vibeshield install with bin null; readiness checks #5–#12 absent from the 79-line src/scan.ts; a real browser Shield Pill/plugin that is CUT; a 'generated' GitHub Action that is hand-committed) and must be explicit, labeled placeholders. The legal-claim matrix and 'not legal advice' discipline are sound. The SPEC/DOCS workstream (docs/pitch-v2/**, tests/pitch-spec/**) is low-risk and non-colliding. Net: SAFE as SPEC_ONLY + DOCS only, in an isolated clone, Mode B rejected for now, all unsupported capabilities labeled placeholders. Not APPROVE_IMPLEMENTATION; not REJECT.
